For New Inside Sales Agents · The Fusion Team

Welcome to the team.

You just joined one of the most-watched real estate teams in New York City. The next five days are going to move fast. We will get you set up, get you trained, and get you on the phone closing appointments by Wednesday. Let's go.

The Brokerage

Founded by Ryan Serhant in 2020. The world's most-followed real estate brand. Featured on Netflix's Owning Manhattan and Bravo's Million Dollar Listing New York. Built different. That matters when you pick up the phone.

Ryan Serhant
Founder & CEO

When you say SERHANT. on a cold call, the seller on the other end already knows the name. They have seen the show. They know the brand. That recognition is the difference between getting hung up on and getting a conversation.

Nearly $10 billion in sales. Star and executive producer of Netflix's Owning Manhattan. Founder of the most-followed real estate brand on earth. That is your unfair advantage. Use it.

Now: meet your team.

SERHANT. is the brokerage. The Fusion Team is who you actually work with every day. Next section.

The Team

Who you'll work with.

The Fusion Team is led by Beatriz Xavier, Founder & Team Lead. Three licensed agents on the front line. Three admin roles keeping the engine running.

The admins handle proposals, comps, property details, listing prep, and back-end coordination. The ISA runs the phones, qualifies leads, and follows up. The agents are the face of the team. We visit clients, present proposals, list properties, negotiate, and close.

Everyone plays a position. The work moves cleanly from one hand to the next because each person owns their part.

02.1 About Beatriz

Beatriz founded The Fusion Team and leads it. A decade in NYC real estate, over $100 million closed, and more than 20 years overall in sales, negotiation, and business development.

Originally from Brazil, she works with local, national, and international clients in three languages: English, Portuguese, and Spanish. Her book includes luxury homeowners, investors, multifamily owners, developers, and high-profile clients who need discretion.

Beatriz specializes in residential sales, luxury properties, multifamily assets, development opportunities, and multimillion-dollar portfolios across Manhattan and Brooklyn. She brings data, negotiation, and creative marketing together to position every listing with purpose.

Before launching The Fusion Team, she spent five years running a productivity coaching program and has mentored hundreds of NYC agents on lead generation, business planning, and deal execution.

She is the broker every qualified appointment routes to.

02.2 About Kale

Kale works rentals and sales across Manhattan's east corridor: Upper East Side, Midtown, all the way down to the Lower East Side.

Analytical approach to every deal, with a strong eye for valuation that comes from his background in insurance and risk. Calm under pressure and patient with clients who need education through the process. Straightforward communicator, transparent on every transaction.

02.3 About Matthew

Matthew focuses on buyers and sellers in downtown Manhattan and Brooklyn. Strong on West Village, Upper East Side, and Brooklyn townhouses.

His listings have been featured in StreetEasy press.

02.4 About Brandon

Queens native. Born and raised in NYC.

Brandon works rentals and condo and co-op sales across Manhattan and Brooklyn. His focus is helping clients build generational wealth through property, with strong work with first-time buyers, investors, and families looking for long-term holdings.

Next: your role.

Now let's get into your part of the machine. What you'll work on day to day, what a great day looks like, and the rhythm we move at together starting week two.

Your Role

Inside Sales Agent.

You are the first voice every seller hears from The Fusion Team. Your job is to listen, qualify, and route the right people to Beatriz at the right moment. The judgment you exercise on every call is the value of this position.

03.1 Scope

In Scope
What you do
  • Call expired-listing leads from the daily call list in FUB
  • Run the SERHANT cold-call script with discipline: opener, qualification, motivation
  • Qualify the seller: is the property real, are they still planning to sell, are you talking to the decision-maker
  • Book qualified appointments directly onto Beatriz's calendar
  • Log every call in FUB in real time, in the standard format
  • Submit a daily report by end of shift: dials, contacts, appointments, hot leads
Out of Scope
What you don't do
  • Pitch terms, commission, or marketing plan, that conversation belongs to Beatriz
  • Promise specific outcomes like price, timing, buyer pool, or days on market
  • Argue with sellers, if they are not selling, disqualify and move on
  • Skip FUB logging because you'll do it later, calls without notes are calls lost
  • Leave voicemails or send texts to leads that did not answer
  • Stay on a call past the point of value, protect your volume

03.2 Daily Role Play

Every morning, 10:00 AM - 10:30 AM: 30-minute role play session, recorded for review. This replaces the previous prep block. The team lead reviews recordings to help you sharpen your delivery.

Twice per month: Mandatory team script practice sessions with the scripts coach and the rest of the team. The team lead will share the schedule.

After the role play, your first call block runs 10:30 AM - 12:30 PM. Everything else in the schedule below shifts accordingly.

03.3 Daily Schedule (EST)

10:00 AM
Log in. Open FUB. Pull today's call list. Review yesterday's notes and flagged follow-ups.
Prep
10:00 to 12:30
First call block. 2.5 hours of focused dialing.
Calling
12:30 to 1:30
Lunch. Full hour. Eat away from your screen.
Break
1:30 to 3:00
Second call block.
Calling
3:00 to 3:15
Break.
Break
3:15 to 5:00
Third call block.
Calling
5:00 to 5:15
Break.
Break
5:15 to 7:00
Final call block. Wrap FUB notes and follow-up tasks.
Calling
By 7:00 PM
Submit daily report. Dials, contacts, appointments, hot leads flagged.
Reporting

8 working hours plus 1 hour lunch. 10am to 7pm EST.

03.4 Rules of Engagement

  • Each lead is called once every 24 hours if they answer
  • Do not leave voicemails. If they did not pick up, log it and move on
  • Do not send texts for unanswered leads
  • You may dial again the same day within an 8-hour gap
  • Double tap every number, two attempts before moving on
  • Cameras on during all team meetings

Next: Your Tools.

Everything you do flows through Follow Up Boss. Watch the videos, learn the workflow, then practice it before your first live call.

Your Tools

Master the platform.

Everything you do flows through four platforms. Master each one before Day 3. Don't just watch the videos. Open your accounts in another tab and follow along.

Follow Up Boss
Your primary CRM. Call list, dialer, notes, routing to the team lead. You're in FUB your entire shift.
Open FUB →
StreetEasy
Where you research the property before each call. 15-second scan. Used 95% of the time since NYC has no MLS.
Open StreetEasy →
Zillow
Backup when the StreetEasy link isn't available. About 5% of the time. Otherwise StreetEasy is the main source.
Open Zillow →
Google Drive
Scripts, lead lists, SOPs. Everything you reference but don't actively work in.
Open Drive →

04.1 Training Videos

Block 01 Organize your workspace.

Video 01 · Finding your call list
ISA Expireds & ISA FSBO

Where your daily work begins. Navigate to the People tab in FUB and find the two smart lists you'll work from every day: ISA Expireds (homes that came off the market without selling) and ISA FSBO (for-sale-by-owner). The team lead decides the sort order each week. Pick the list, follow that order, start at the top.

Video 02 · What you'll see inside a lead
Reading the lead profile

Open a lead and there's a lot on the page. Name, every phone number, email, the StreetEasy link, the Background section, and previous notes from anyone who has called this person before. Before you dial, open the StreetEasy link and read any prior notes. Two things, every time.

Block 02 Make the call.

Video 03 · FUB Dialer (with call notes)
Running the list back-to-back

When you want to push volume, the FUB built-in dialer runs through your list automatically. This video covers how to launch the dialer, how to log each call in real time, and the standard note format: [last 4 of phone] [what happened]. Example: 4892 spoke to Rod, $1.5M ask, tenant 1yr, motivated.

Video 04 · Manual calls, double tap & notes
The fundamental call workflow

Dial manually one lead at a time. Double-tap every number: call once, no answer, immediately call again. Many sellers pick up on the second ring. Use the notes field to prep yourself in the 5 seconds before they answer, and type live during the call. Don't wait until after to write things down.

Block 03 Disposition & route.

Video 05 · Assigning calls
ISA Leads Follow Up vs ISA Reject

The moment a call ends, you route the lead through the Assigned To field. Two destinations only: ISA Leads Follow Up if you reached the right person and they're a real prospect, or ISA Reject if they've already signed with another broker or are firmly not selling. If they haven't signed yet, leave the assignment so the team lead can try.

Video 06 · Research Tag
When all numbers are wrong

Sometimes every phone number on a lead is for the wrong person. Don't give up and don't move to ISA Reject. Mark the lead with the Research tag. The team will find correct numbers and put the lead back in your queue.

Block 04 Research & report.

Video 07 · Reading StreetEasy listings
15-second property scan

Before each call, open the StreetEasy link on the lead. Scan for price history, days on market, photo quality, and whether the listing has been relisted. This 15-second scan tells you whether you're dealing with a realistic seller or someone in denial about their price. It also gives you a specific opening line beyond "Hi, I'm calling about your property."

Video 08 · Hourly tracking, EOD report & WhatsApp confirmations
Reporting and appointment confirmations

Three things this video covers. First: hourly tracking throughout your shift so the team sees your numbers in real time, not just at end of day. Second: the end-of-day report you submit before logging off. Third: the WhatsApp message you send after booking an appointment to confirm with the seller. All three are non-negotiable.

Knowledge Check

10 questions on the training above.

Answer all 10 to see your score. If you score below 80%, rewatch the videos linked next to each missed question, then retake the quiz before Day 3.

Question 01
Where do you find your call list in FUB?
Question 02
When you open a lead in FUB, before you dial, what should you check?
Question 03
When the seller doesn't pick up, what do you do?
Question 04
You log a call. What's the correct note format?
Question 05
A seller answered and said: "I already signed with my previous agent yesterday." Where do you route them?
Question 06
A seller said: "I'm thinking about going back to my previous agent, but I haven't signed anything yet." What do you do?
Question 07
A motivated seller wants to meet the team lead. After booking the appointment, what else must you do?
Question 08
You've tried every phone number on a lead and they all belong to wrong people. What do you do?
Question 09
Before calling a seller about their property, where do you research the listing?
Question 10
When do you report your numbers?
0/10
Your Score

04.2 ISA Best Practices

These are the rules that keep your work clean, the database accurate, and the team's reputation intact. Read them before you start calling. Reference them whenever you're not sure what to do.

  1. Call each lead no more than once per day.
  2. If the lead does not answer:
    1. Do NOT leave voicemails under any circumstances.
    2. Do NOT send text messages for unanswered leads.
    3. You may call again on the same day within an 8-hour period.
  3. Double-tap every number. Call once and immediately call again if there is no answer.
  4. If the client mentioned they are on the DNC list or they already SIGNED with another broker, put notes and move it to ISA Reject.
  5. Schedule appointments with the team Monday through Sunday between 9:00 AM and 12:00 PM.
  6. If any issue occurs during the call attempt, record it in the notes.
  7. Always log the last 4 digits of the phone number and describe what happened on each call attempt in the notes.
  8. Call every phone number listed for every contact.
  9. If a phone number appears to be an incorrect person, mark it with the Research tag and we will find the correct numbers.
  10. Cameras on during all team meetings.

Next: Script Mastery.

You know the platform. Time to learn what to say. Five segments: the framework, the scripts, two practice calls, and a recorded conversation from our team that converted.

Script Mastery

Master your voice.

Five segments cover the framework, the scripts, voice practice, and a recorded call from our team. Open the practice module in a new tab and work through it section by section.

The phone is the job. Everything in here gets you ready for it.

01
Framework

The four-beat call structure from the scripts coach. Opener, gather, position, close.

02
Short Script

The 30-second version. Master this before you touch the full playbook.

03
Full Expireds Playbook

Every objection. Every handle. Built around the Phil M Jones magic words.

04
Voice Mock Call

Practice live with the browser microphone. AI plays the seller. You handle the call.

05
Recorded Call from Our Team

A real call with our annotations. Hear what good sounds like before you dial.

Open Script Practice →

Opens in a new tab. Headphones recommended.

10.2 Appointment Confirmation

Once an appointment is booked on the team lead's calendar, the appointment is not done. Confirmation closes the loop. No-shows cost the team real money.

01
Send WhatsApp confirmation within 1 hour of booking
Use the team WhatsApp template. The team lead will share the template directly with you.
02
Send a 24-hour reminder
The day before the appointment, send a second WhatsApp message confirming the time, address, and the team lead's name.
03
Log both confirmations in FUB notes
Add a note for each message sent: WA confirm sent [date] and WA 24hr reminder sent [date].
04
If they don't respond to the reminder, call them
A confirmed appointment is the goal. If the 24-hour reminder gets no reply by end of day, call to confirm verbally before close of business.

10.3 ISA Best Practices

Same rules as on Your Tools. Reprinted here for quick reference.

  1. Call each lead no more than once per day.
  2. If the lead does not answer:
    1. Do NOT leave voicemails under any circumstances.
    2. Do NOT send text messages for unanswered leads.
    3. You may call again on the same day within an 8-hour period.
  3. Double-tap every number. Call once and immediately call again if there is no answer.
  4. If the client mentioned they are on the DNC list or they already SIGNED with another broker, put notes and move it to ISA Reject.
  5. Schedule appointments with the team Monday through Sunday between 9:00 AM and 12:00 PM.
  6. If any issue occurs during the call attempt, record it in the notes.
  7. Always log the last 4 digits of the phone number and describe what happened on each call attempt in the notes.
  8. Call every phone number listed for every contact.
  9. If a phone number appears to be an incorrect person, mark it with the Research tag and we will find the correct numbers.
  10. Cameras on during all team meetings.
